fields.description:
## GHA reconcile-bridge: sign inbound writes inline + inject key

CRITICAL: the reconciler's inbound writer (`_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py`
`_write_event_file`, ~214-259) calls the `rebar._store` primitives DIRECTLY, bypassing
`_seam.append_event`, and stamps only `author` (no `author_email`/`author_id`/`author_sig`).
So Jira-sourced events classify `unknown-author`/`unsigned` under `verify-identity`. Config
alone is insufficient â€” this needs a code fix.

### Chosen approach: stamp + sign INLINE (not via `_seam.append_event`)

Enrich the event dict in `_write_event_file` BEFORE it is serialized/written:
1. `event.update(_seam.attribution_fields(repo_root))` â€” adds `author_email` and (when a
   current identity resolves) `author_id`.
2. `_seam._apply_authorship(event, ticket_id, event_type, data, str(tracker_dir), repo_root)`
   â€” when `author_id` + `identity.signing_key` are present, signs the canonical event bytes
   and stores the DSSE envelope under `author_sig` (best-effort; a miss writes unsigned).

**Rationale (why inline, NOT `_seam.append_event`):** `append_event` calls `write_and_push()`,
which COMMITS + PUSHES per event; `_write_event_file` intentionally writes an uncommitted file
so the reconciler's own orchestration can batch-commit the pass. Routing through
`append_event` would break that batch-commit model and thread `repo_root`/`tracker` through
12+ call sites. Inline stamping reuses the exact seam helpers, preserves the uncommitted-file
model, and is minimal blast radius. The added author fields are additive (older readers ignore
unknown keys â€” no store-format break). `_write_event_file` will need `repo_root` available
(derive from `tracker_dir.parent` or `config.repo_root()`).

### Prerequisite (SATISFIED)

Signing resolves `author_id` only if a Rebar Bot identity ticket with
`email=joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` exists â€” it does (task bffe created identity
`594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`, public key registered). So with `BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL` = that address the
git-email resolver maps reconciler writes to the bot identity.

### Workflow wiring

In `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`: write `secrets.REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` to a
job-local file (`mktemp`, `chmod 600`, `::add-mask::` the value), set `identity.signing_key`
(or `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`) to that path, and set `BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL=joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`
(currently `rebar-bridge@users.noreply.github.com`) so git-email resolution maps to the bot.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_write_event_file` stamps `author_email`/`author_id` (via `_seam.attribution_fields`) and signs `author_sig` (via `_seam._apply_authorship`) inline before writing; a reconciler-authored event written with the bot identity + a signing key verifies as `verified` under `rebar verify-authorship` (proven by a unit test that configures a bot identity + signing key, drives `_write_event_file`, and asserts the written event carries `author_id`/`author_email`/`author_sig` and verifies).
- [ ] Additive-fields regression test: a unit test constructs an event WITHOUT the new author fields (older-format) and asserts the reducer / `rebar show` still round-trips it cleanly (unknown/absent keys ignored, no format break). The batch-commit orchestration is unchanged (no eager commit/push added to the inbound path).
- [ ] `reconcile-bridge.yml` writes the key to a `chmod 600` job-local file, `::add-mask::`s it, sets `identity.signing_key` to that path, and sets `BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL=joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`; the key is never logged or committed.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
